data.body:
DESIGN DECISION — APPROVED: base->overlay escalation mechanism (reviewer-selection layer)

Overlay activation = UNION of two sources:
  overlays_to_run = { overlays whose deterministic triggers (applies_to globs / content signatures) match the diff }
                  UNION { overlays the base reviewer escalated to }

Mechanism:
1. Round A (parallel): the BASE reviewer ALWAYS runs (every change) alongside deterministically-triggered overlays. The base emits its pass-1 findings AND a bounded escalation signal: recommend_overlays: [{overlay_id, reason}], where overlay_id is constrained to the FIXED catalog enum (base cannot invent an overlay; one-line concrete reason required, same abstention discipline as findings).
2. A DETERMINISTIC orchestrator takes the union and dispatches any escalated overlays not already run in Round A.
3. Round B (parallel): those escalated overlays run their pass-1 evidence contract.
4. ALL findings (base + Round-A + Round-B overlays) flow into the SAME pass-2 binary-verifier + pass-3 deterministic typed gate.

Safety / why this does not reintroduce "the LLM decides":
- Escalation only decides WHICH advisory reviewers run; it cannot affect the verdict. A false escalation costs one extra reviewer pass (not a wrong gate); a missed escalation is a recall gap backstopped by the deterministic trigger union; an overlay's findings still pass through pass-2 verification + pass-3 deterministic gate, so escalation can never bypass the block logic or force a finding through. Escalation lives on the recall/selection side (model-influence acceptable); the precision/decision side stays deterministic.

Bounds:
- ONE HOP, no recursion: base escalates to overlays; overlays do NOT re-escalate (prevents specialist->specialist cascades).
- Per-change CAP on number of overlays dispatched (e.g. top-N by base's stated confidence) so a noisy diff doesn't fan out to all overlays.
- Conservative by prompt: escalate only with a concrete reason.

Where it lives: extends the existing reviewer-selection seam (select_code_reviewers in code_review.py) into two stages — Stage 1 deterministic (base + glob/content-matched overlays); Stage 2 escalation (after base returns, union in base's recommend_overlays, dispatch any not already run). Per-overlay escalation cues are recorded in the OVERLAY CATALOG comments on this epic (e.g. performance "introduced-N+1 has no signature", api-compat "same-signature behavior change", db "ORM model changed without a migration", a11y "interactive/ARIA/focus in any file", i18n "hardcoded user-facing strings without i18n"). Performance is the most escalation-reliant overlay (weakest deterministic triggers).

Status: APPROVED / locked. To be reflected as a reviewer-selection acceptance criterion when E2 is built. (No implementation yet.)
